title: Convert MCP_TOOL_MAP loopbacks to DIRECT_HANDLERS and strip legacy MCP tools
description:
# Story: Convert all MCP_TOOL_MAP loopback entries to DIRECT_HANDLERS, then strip every legacy Plexus MCP tool registration so the server exposes a single MCP tool: `execute_tactus`.

## Why
- The Plexus MCP server should be the single-tool runtime architecture end-to-end. `execute_tactus` is the public surface; the other ~60 tools are now legacy.
- `MCP_TOOL_MAP` (in `MCP/tools/tactus_runtime/execute.py`) currently routes 18 Tactus calls back through registered FastMCP tools (in-process loopback). As long as those entries exist, the legacy tool registrations cannot be removed.
- Per project rule: every method has exactly one dispatcher (`DIRECT_HANDLERS` comment already states this). `MCP_TOOL_MAP` is a dual code path — to be eliminated.
- Removing the legacy registrations also drops the slow imports they pull in (langchain, matplotlib, score-node Pydantic models), shortening cold start and eliminating the Cursor connect-timeout race.

## End state
- `MCP/plexus_fastmcp_server.py` registers exactly one tool: `execute_tactus`.
- No call to `register_*_tools(mcp)` other than `register_tactus_tools(mcp)`.
- `MCP_TOOL_MAP` is empty (or removed).
- All Tactus runtime APIs (scorecards/score/item/feedback/evaluation/dataset/report/procedure/handle/docs/api) work via `DIRECT_HANDLERS` only.
- `get_plexus_documentation` and `think` tools removed (docs accessible via `plexus.docs.*` inside `execute_tactus`).
- Cold start measurably faster; no `Component already exists` warnings.

## Migration slices (each becomes a sub-task)
1. Fix double-registration in plexus_fastmcp_server.py
2. scorecards.list/info -> DIRECT_HANDLERS
3. score.info/evaluations/predict/set_champion -> DIRECT_HANDLERS
4. item.info/last -> DIRECT_HANDLERS
5. feedback.alignment -> DIRECT_HANDLERS
6. evaluation.find_recent/compare -> DIRECT_HANDLERS
7. dataset.build_from_feedback_window/check_associated -> DIRECT_HANDLERS
8. report.configurations_list -> DIRECT_HANDLERS
9. procedure.list/info/chat_sessions/chat_messages -> DIRECT_HANDLERS
10. Strip legacy registrations and think/get_plexus_documentation. Verify with live MCP stdio smoke test.

## Acceptance
- tools/list over MCP returns one entry: execute_tactus.
- All previously verified Tactus snippets still work end-to-end.
- Existing unit tests pass; new direct-handler tests added per slice.
- Cold start improved; no duplicate registration warnings.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Decision: Path A (preserve runtime APIs). Will convert all MCP_TOOL_MAP entries to DIRECT_HANDLERS, then delete legacy MCP tools entirely. Slice 1 starting: scorecards.list/info.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: All MCP_TOOL_MAP loopbacks converted to DIRECT_HANDLERS. Legacy tool catalog eliminated — server now exposes only execute_tactus. console/chat_agent.tac fixed to work with the new single-tool architecture: extract_text updated for Lupa userdata, MessageHistory auto-load removed to prevent context window explosion, system prompt updated with evaluation.run/procedure.optimize examples, response truncation added (40K cap), budget auto-default for embedded MCP context, and execute_tactus now dispatches procedure.optimize in background thread for immediate response. Verified end-to-end via programmatic tests: L1a (scorecard list), L1b (score info), L2 (trigger evaluation → real eval ID), L3 (start optimization → procedure ID in ~49s). Story is functionally complete. Closing.
